summary: DHH reflects on his first month studying economics, describing it as a mind-altering experience that has improved his thinking across all domains. He argues that economic frameworks serve as powerful mental filters for understanding business, government, and society. He dismisses concerns about whether the knowledge will be directly applicable to his dream career, concluding that the intellectual benefits alone justify continued study.
key_points: Studying economics has improved systematic thinking skills beyond just the economic domain
Economic theories act as noise filters that reveal signal in daily world events
Understanding business and government motivations simplifies processing complex societal changes
Direct career applicability shouldn't be the sole measure of whether knowledge is worth pursuing
money_quotes: Economic patterns and theories makes comprehending society easier. They're tools for filtering out noise and focussing on the signal.
Understanding the motivations of businesses and governments reduce the complexities of absorbing the daily flow of worldly changes.
Concerns on whether these tools will be directly applicable to the job of my dreams shouldn't be allowed to hog brain cycles.
They're easing life in a commercial world, revealing connections previously hidden, and jump-starting great thinking.
That's plenty of justification for pushing further.
key_insight: Broad intellectual frameworks like economics pay dividends beyond their direct domain by sharpening systematic thinking and filtering noise from signal in everyday life.
